title: Internal names do not match external names
body:
We refer to the pause_image and pause_container in the libpod.conf
description, but internally we had infra_image and infra_container.

This meand it the user made changes to the conf, it would not effect the
actual tool using libpod.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
This breaks existing config files for people actually using them. We should change the manpages if anything.
LGTM, but would like a head nod from @mheon

@mheon Well if anyone was using the documented names then they will start working, right now the names are ignored.  

I don't see any reason why a user would have been using the internal names.

```
infra_image = "k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.1"
infra_command = "/pause"
```

Are in every config file we autogenerate for Rootless - I think we've established the convention already that these are named `infra_`
@vrothberg can you give it the magic /lgtm

> @vrothberg can you give it the magic /lgtm

Wanted @mheon's pair of eyes on it that seemed to have caught something here. So it seems that performing this change would allow root to work which was previously broken BUT it will break rootless which is currently working.

IMHO we should change the man page (and the libpod.conf?) to use `infra_` to avoid recursing into a root <-> rootless ping-pong.

@mheon @rhatdan WDYT?
As the person that introduced this inconsistency many months ago, I think using infra* is more accurate
@haircommander @vrothberg Ok went the other way and also found a crio.conf file we were carrying for no reason so removed it.
@haircommander @mheon Can I get a /lgtm on this one?
